block_01: We noticed that you haven’t checked on your trading account lately. If you’re lacking motivation, try a new approach — for example, a simple, single-indicator trading strategy.
block_10: The strategy is based on using just one indicator — Simple Moving Average (SMA).
block_11: A golden cross happens when a short-term SMA crosses over a long-term SMA. It is considered a bullish sign.
block_12: When a short-term SMA crosses below the long-term one, it's called a "death cross" and considered a bearish sign.
block_15: Use two SMA indicators with different period settings: 50 (short-term) and 200 (long-term). For short trades, feel free to set 5 and 15.
block_16: Golden Cross: short-term SMA crosses the long-term one from below and both of them rise → the price is likely to rise.
block_17: Death Cross: short-term SMA crosses the long-term SMA from above and both of them fall → the price is likely to fall as well.
block_18: Open the BUY trade in the case of Golden Cross and SELL trade in the case of Death Cross.
block_20: Example of successful trend trading using SMA indicator
